{{DISPLAYTITLE: BRC-SFC}} The '''BRC-SFC''' ''BRC-TZ'' (Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium-Small Arms
Synchronization; [[Aurielleno]]: Buseleum Cowensorcio Resorase-Steco Fiuscil Cesueat) is an Auriellen pistol-caliber carbine chambered for the 9.0x18mm Aurum cartridge developed by the state-owned small arms research and development manufacturer Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium based in Nereneavos. The carbine was designed in synchronization with the Pistol Standardization Project initialized in 2011, and inital test trials of the carbine began in 2012 amongst the bases of [[Level 340]] with testing completed near the end of 2013. Although originally intended for use with Auriellen security forces, the weapon has also seen usage with support troops in the [[Republic of Aurielle#Military|Auriellen military]], notably combat engineers and logistic drivers. == Development == Although the Auriellen military has a large and diverse supply of service rifles chambered for intermediate cartridges, the military lacked a central weapon to fit the role of a submachine gun or roles where intermediate cartridges are deemed too powerful. The Buseleum Reconnaissance Consortium (BRC) was assigned to develop a new weapon to fit the role (designated as the 'SFC'), and the initial design stages of the weapon began in 2010. During this period, the Auriellen military also lacked a centralized pistol cartridge, making logistics more difficult, especially with pilots who were armed with various incompatible submachine guns and pistols. Thus the Pistol Standardization Project (PSP) was initialized in 2011 in order to develop a new standard pistol cartridge for use with Auriellen forces and their allies. Because the SFC project was in it's earliest stages, the BRC decided to merge the two projects, developing what would become the BRC-SFC along with a new cartridge, 9.0x18 Aurum, which the SFC would be chambered specifically for. The 9.0x18 Aurum is essentially a stubbier, thicker version of the 9x19 Parabellum round, designed with reduced focus on armor penetration due to the reduced presence of body armor within the Wake. Compared to traditional 9x19 Parabellum, 9.0x18 Aurum has reduced effectiveness against armor but overall higher effectiveness at subduing targets at close range due to its higher mass and internal pressure, an exchange deemed acceptable for the purposes of the cartridge for close ranges as a defense tool. The recoil of the round is low similar to its parent cartridge; it is possible to stably fire the weapon with one arm with decent accuracy. An additional 'Sov Puwer' (SP) round was also created, essentially the base 9.0x18 Aurum round but with even higher pressures, improving it's penetrative ability and somewhat reducing recoil at the downside of putting additional strain on the weapon's critical parts. == Design Details == === Operating Mechanism === The BRC-SFC uses a straight-blowback operation and fires from a closed bolt position. When fired, gas pressure drives the case back in the chamber against the resistance provided by the weight of the bolt and its two recoil springs. The bolt travels back, extracting the empty case which is then ejected straight upwards through a port in the receiver housing top cover. The weapon utilizes a telescoping bolt, allowing for a much smaller assembly which wraps around the base of the barrel. The weapon features a fire selector on the left side which can switch between safe, semi-automatic, and automatic fire modes. The ‘safe’ setting forces the bolt into the forward position and disables the trigger. The stock BRC-SFC has a cyclic fire rate of 750 RPM, restricted by the inertial rate limiter contained within the weapon’s grip. When the bolt reaches the end of its rearward stroke it strikes and is caught by a spring-powered hook mounted on the back plate. At the same time, it drives a lightweight, spring-loaded plunger down into the pistol grip. The plunger is easily accelerated and passes through a heavy weight which is left behind because of its inertia. The plunger, having compressed its spring, is driven up again and then meets the descending inertia buffer. This slows down the rising plunger which, when it reaches the top of its travel, rotates the hook, releasing the bolt which is driven forward by the compressed recoil springs. By adjusting the compression of the spring-loaded plunger, one is able to reduce the carbine’s fire rate even further to as low as 600 or even 500 RPM, usually for more stable recoil control. Removing the limiter entirely can allow the carbine to fire at a rate upwards of 1050 RPM, although this typically is not used due to the weapon becoming significantly harder to control outside of short bursts. The BRC-SFC features a semi-straight grip which is connected to the remainder of the stock. The stock itself is built with a lowered comb designed to compliment the weapon's iron sights. The stock can be detached from the grip, allowing the weapon to be used easier in environments where a smaller weapon is preferred. === Ammunition === The BRC-SFC uses the 9.0x18 Aurum cartridge with 10 and 20 round box magazines. 30 round magazines exist but are less common and are not used in standard service. It is possible to convert the BRC-SFC to fire 9x19mm Parabellum, .380 ACP, and 7.65x17mm Browning Short with slight modifications to the receiver and a barrel replacement. The Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium additionally offers 10, 20, and 30 round magazines for 9x19mm Parabellum, but does not offer magazines for any other calibers. === Accessories === The stock of the BRC-SFC is hollow and can be opened with a sliding latch. The empty space is typically used to store cleaning kits, oil bottles, adjustment tools, or spare magazines (specifically the 10 round variant). == Variants == The BRC-SFC was originally designed to be used as its sole variant, although an alternate ‘assault’ variant was designed. The main differences between the assault variant and the main variant are: * The assault variant had a single articulated under-folding stock rather than the fixed stock of the standard version. The folding stock connected to the grip of the carbine and when folded connected to a hook located directly above the magwell. * The assault variant was specifically designed to use the SP variant of the 9.0x18mm round, rather than the standard pressure version. * The assault variant had different iron sights than the standard versions, having a raised ghost ring aperture sight. The variant, introduced in 2017, was intended to be used for offensive combat, rather than the defensive purposes of the original weapon. The weapon was rendered obsolete when compared to the full-size rifles in use by the Auriellen military and thus never reached full-scale production. It is estimated that around 500 of these carbines were created, although production has since ceased and the weapon is no longer offered. In 2016, a civilian version of the BRC-SFC was designed. Compared to the base weapon, the civilian BRC-SFC lacks the capability for fully-automatic fire and cannot detach its stock. This version of the weapon is built in both the native 9.0x18mm Aurielle cartridge and 9x19mm Parabellum. == Usage == In mid-2014, the Auriellen military placed an order for 1,200 instances of the BRC-SFC carbine for the partial replacement of non-handgun pistol-caliber weapons ('unstandardized' weapons, or larger weapons which did not use the newly developed caliber) as part of the PSP project. In September of 2015, Aurielle ordered an additional 2,400 BRC-SFCs for the total replacement of the unstandardized weapons, effectively completing the program. The weapon was trialed by the [[Duchy of Cascada]] in [[Level 107]] but was ultimately not adopted due to difficulties with the weapon's handling in colder environments. In early 2018, large amounts of the civilian version of the carbine were imported into [[Level 79]], although how the specifically the weapons arrived there is unknown, as the Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium claims to have had no awareness of the importation and denies involvement with it. The civilian variant of the BRC-SFC is known to be widespread as a home-defense weapon in [[Level 79]] as claimed by interviewees. === Conflicts === * [[Cascadan Conflict]] (both the standard and assault variants of the weapon were used) * [[Cascadan Emergency|Cascadan Civil War]] * [[Incident 154: Part I|Incident 154]]
